purpose: Measurement-only estimate of Edax level-10 vs level-16 labeling time for ~4.43M WTHOR positions; no production code changed, no generation job started.

productionLv16Actuals.note: Real production wall-clock elapsedMs per record, aggregated by record.empties. 0 mixed-level records confirmed (childEmpties = parentEmpties - 1 is constant per record, so level is fully determined by parentEmpties).

liveAbSample.environment: quiet (no other heavy processes), 16 logical CPUs, otherwise idle

keyFindingNonMonotonicLevel.description: level=10 is 5.1x-9.6x faster than level=16 for parent empties 22-30 (the historically 'heavy band'), but 2.4x-4.1x SLOWER than level=16 for parent empties 31-59, despite level=10 always reporting a shallower search depth (10 vs 16, verified via Edax -vv depth output, no exact auto-promotion occurred). A uniform level 16->10 switch is therefore a net time INCREASE, not decrease.

scopeNote: No production code (gen_teacher_corpus.py, vs_edax.py) was modified. No 4.43M-record labeling job was started. Measurement scripts (analyze_corpus.py, sample_positions.py, sample_sensitivity.py, measure_ab.py) were kept in the session scratchpad and are not part of this commit.
